question_text: A hydraulic lift system has two cylinders. The input cylinder has an area of 0.5 m² and the output cylinder has an area of 8 m². A force of 200 N is applied to the input cylinder. Calculate the force produced at the output cylinder, then determine how many times larger this output force is compared to the input force.
correct_answer: Output force = 3200 N; amplification factor = 16 times
explanation: Using the principle that pressure is transmitted equally through a hydraulic system, pressure = force/area. First, calculate input pressure: 200 N / 0.5 m² = 400 Pa. Then use this pressure on the output cylinder: force = 400 Pa × 8 m² = 3200 N. The force is amplified 3200 / 200 = 16 times because the output area is 16 times larger.
